sanskrit:
अहं वैश्वानरो भूत्वा प्राणिनां देहमाश्रितः।
प्राणापानसमायुक्तः पचाम्यन्नं चतुर्विधम्॥
transliteration:
ahaṃ vaiśvānaro bhūtvā prāṇināṃ deham āśritaḥ |
prāṇāpāna-samāyuktaḥ pacāmy annaṃ catur-vidham ||
literal_meaning: I, having become the universal fire (Vaiśvānara), dwell in the bodies of living beings; united with the incoming and outgoing breaths, I digest the four kinds of food.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reveals that the same Consciousness which pervades the cosmos is quietly metabolizing every experience—physical, emotional, mental—within the individual. Digestion is not merely biological; it is the divine alchemy of turning raw experience into usable life-energy.

shadow: Fear of being consumed by life

psychological_state: A felt sense that the body is not an obstacle but a sacred vessel in which the Divine is already at work, metabolizing every ‘bite’ of life into wisdom.
trigger_scenario: After a stress-eating episode you place a hand on your belly, feel the warmth, and realize the same intelligence that turns sandwich into heartbeat is also turning heartbreak into insight.
anchor_text: Feel every experience being alchemized into life-energy within you.
